top_signals.title: Treasuries Volatility Will Go Higher: Marden
top_signals.summary: Adam Marden, co-portfolio manager of the dynamic global bond strategy at T. Rowe Price, and Deirdre Dunn, head of global rates at Citi, join Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Treasuries gained after the Federal Reserve’s favored inflation gauge rose less than estimated, damping expectations for an interest-rate hike in the months ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: We Have Seen Peak Inflation: Stuart Paul
top_signals.summary: Bloomberg Economics' Stuart Paul joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." US consumer spending accelerated in May even as prices rose at the fastest pace in more than three years, suggesting Americans are powering through the fallout from the Iran war. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump Seeks $88 Billion for Iran War
top_signals.summary: President Donald Trump asked Congress to pass a supplemental spending package to backfill the cost of the war in Iran and respond to public health crises abroad. Steve Ellis, President of Taxpayers for Common Sense joins Balance of Power to discuss the supplemental funding request.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
top_signals.summary: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: China detains two Japanese nationals over alleged rare earth smuggling - Financial Times
top_signals.summary: China detains two Japanese nationals over alleged rare earth smuggling  Financial Times
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Micron earnings, oil prices fall, the newest meme stock and more in Morning Squawk
top_signals.summary: Here are five key things investors need to know to start the trading day.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Moderna CEO: Cautiously Optimistic on Flu Vaccine Approval
top_signals.summary: The FDA advisory committee recommended Moderna's flu vaccine for approval. Moderna CEO Stéphane Bancel tells Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld he is cautiously optimistic the approval will happen.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Jair Bolsonaro’s wife accuses stepson of ‘stab in the back’ in election feud
top_signals.summary: Former first lady Michelle lashes out at presidential candidate Flávio in social media video in latest setback to campaign
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Live special: Ten years after Brexit
U.S. International Transactions and Investment Position, 1st Quarter 2026 and Annual Update
Remarks by Commissioner Hoekstra at the ENVI Council
top_signals.summary:
European Commission Speech Luxembourg, 25 Jun 2026 Ladies and gentlemen,
Very good evening, I have been thanking you more often for your tenacity. Thank you very much for being here.
This morning at the doorstep...
